---
title: "Track Your Training Smarter - Data"
---

:::{=html}
<link rel="stylesheet" href="https://cdnjs.cloudflare.com/ajax/libs/font-awesome/6.5.1/css/all.min.css" integrity="sha512-9usAa8m0M+WyW59Ry...cut..." crossorigin="anonymous" referrerpolicy="no-referrer" />
:::

<div class="project-trail">
  <a href="../training_log.qmd">Training Log</a>
</div>

Before diving into analysis, it's worth showing where the numbers come from. I wanted a consistent and automated way to capture my training. 

Two main datasets are daily updated and live in Google Sheets. Having the data in this format makes it easy to explore manually, but also gives me a flexible base for deeper analysis. I provide only a static sample of data logs.

- [Activity Log](https://docs.google.com/spreadsheets/d/1o5Y9_AM_8baj5DnB2AE9nYXU_ZSPsyFW6VjRbljSWIw/edit?gid=412740509#gid=412740509){target="_blank"} - training activities with different metrics, including distance, duration, elevation, heart rate. 

<div class="sheet-embed-container">
  <iframe 
    src="https://docs.google.com/spreadsheets/d/1o5Y9_AM_8baj5DnB2AE9nYXU_ZSPsyFW6VjRbljSWIw/preview?gid=412740509#gid=412740509"
    class="sheet-embed">
  </iframe>
</div>

- [Daily Log](https://docs.google.com/spreadsheets/d/1k-JIYE5z9a-2IqcslkliL_Y6CdvcNeZ7NoVe8wDbo8I/edit?gid=0#gid=0){target="_blank"} - day-level statistics, such as steps, sleep, resting heart rate, recovery time, and other daily metrics.

<div class="sheet-embed-container">
  <iframe 
    src="https://docs.google.com/spreadsheets/d/1k-JIYE5z9a-2IqcslkliL_Y6CdvcNeZ7NoVe8wDbo8I/preview?gid=0#gid=0"
    class="sheet-embed">
  </iframe>
</div>

The statistics come from Garmin Connect. A Python script fetches both activity and daily statistics, and then pushes them into the Google Sheets. Some metrics are raw Garmin values, while others are adjusted or calculated. Statistics that are captured in sheets can also evolve over time as we discover new metrics or further analyze training. So, some of the statistics in the sheet can also be presented in detail in other blog posts.

The script is run each morning and spreadsheets expand by one more day of history (yesterday's data), keeping the logs synchronized without manual work. The script can also loop through multiple accounts if provided.

::: {.post-note}
<div class="note-label">Note | July 2026 | garminconnect external dependency</div>

`garminconnect` is an unofficial wrapper around Garmin Connect, so login behavior can change outside our control. After GitHub Actions started hitting `too many 429 error responses`, we moved away from asking Garmin for a fresh email/password login on every scheduled run. The daily job now reuses a saved Garmin session token that was created locally and stored securely in GitHub Secrets, while keeping email/password login as a backup if the saved session stops working.
:::

The script relies on two key integrations:

- `Garmin Connect API` - accessed through the unofficial Python `garminconnect` library (`garminconnect==0.3.6`), which is used to fetch activities and health metrics.
```python
import os
from garminconnect import Garmin  

garminClient = Garmin(garmin_email, garmin_password)  

if garmin_tokenstore:
    garminClient.login(tokenstore=garmin_tokenstore)
else:
    garminClient.login()

garminClient.get_activities_by_date(date_from, date_to)
```

- `Google Sheets API` - used to store and update logs. The script uses `gspread==6.1.4` as the Python client for Google Sheets, and `google-auth==2.38.0` to authenticate with a service account through `Credentials`.
```python
import gspread
from google.oauth2.service_account import Credentials  

drive_credentials = Credentials.from_service_account_file(
    "googleDrive_secrets.json",  
    scopes=["https://www.googleapis.com/auth/spreadsheets",  
            "https://www.googleapis.com/auth/drive"]  
)

googleDrive_client = gspread.authorize(drive_credentials)  
googleDrive_client.open(user_dailyLogFilename)
```

Of course, logging in requires credentials (emails, passwords, API keys). They are not hardcoded, but live in a *.env* file locally and as GitHub Secrets in the cloud, keeping sensitive content secure while still accessible for automation.

```{=html}
<div class="project-link-container">
    <i class="fab fa-github"></i>
    <span> You can find the full code for this section, which is then easily integrated into the <code>src/main.py</code> script, on my Github repository under the
    <a href="https://github.com/1312Bravo/TrainingPeaks_customLog/tree/main/basic_daily_activity_statistics" target="_blank"> basic_daily_activity_statistics</a>
     directory.</span>
</div>
```

::: {.post-nav}
[Back to Training Log](../training_log.qmd){.post-nav-back}
:::
